questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: If you are a classical singer, we have a challenging job for you.
Assumptions: I. We need a classical singer.
II. You are a classical singer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The successful man has the ability to judge himself correctly.
Assumptions: I. To judge others is of no use to a successful man.
II. The successful man cannot make a wrong judgment.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "You should not grant leave this week because of exigency of work." - A supervisor advises the administrative officer.
Assumptions: I. Request for leave can even be turned down.
II. The supervisor has reviewed the work required to be done during the said period.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The function will start at 3 p.m. You are requested to take your seats before 3 p.m." - last sentence in an invitation card.
Assumptions: I. If the invitee is not in his seat before 3 p.m., the function will not start.
II. The function will start as scheduled.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Use PVC pipes which have life years longer than any other.
Assumptions: I. People prefer those pipes which are durable.
II. Other pipes are not durable.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "You are hereby appointed as a programmer with a probation period of one year and your performance will be reviewed at the end of the period for confirmation." - a line in an appointment letter
Assumptions: I. The performance of an individual is generally not known at the time of appointment offer.
II. Generally, an individual tries to prove his worth in the probation period.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Lock your valuables in a cupboard and call everybody a petty thief.
Assumptions: I. Valuables locked in a cupboard cannot be stolen.
II. Stealing is a crime.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Ensure a good night's sleep for your family with safe and effective X mosquito coil." - an advertisement
Assumptions: I. X mosquito coil is better than any other mosquito coil.
II. A good night's sleep is desirable.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Lack of stimulation in the first four or five years of life can have adverse consequences.
Assumptions: I. A great part of the development of observed intelligence occurs in the earliest years of life.
II. 50 per cent of the measurable intelligence at age 17 is predictable by the age of four.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The present examination system needs thorough overhauling.
Assumptions: I. The present examination system is obsolete.
II. Overhauling results in improvement.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The improvement in the quality of TV programmes will lead to increase in the sales of TV.
Assumptions: I. TV is a good entertainment medium.
II. The quality of TV programmes has improved recently.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Banking services are fine-tuned to meet the growing business needs." - an advertisement
Assumptions: I. Banking is a part of business activity.
II. Industrialists prefer better banking services.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Vitamin C tablets keep work complexion in a glowing condition by improving circulation.
Assumptions: I. People like a glowing complexion.
II. Complexion becomes dull in the absence of circulation.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "We offer the best training in the field of computers." — an advertisement
Assumptions: I. People are interested in getting training in computers.
II. People want the best training.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Please put more people on the job but make up for the delay."
Assumptions: I. Delay is inevitable in most jobs.
II. The output will increase with more number of people on the job.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: An advertisement in a newspaper - "Wanted unmarried, presentable matriculate girls between 18 and 21, able to speak fluently in English, to be hired as models."
Assumptions: I. Fluency in English is a prerequisite for good performance as a model.
II. Light does not matter in performing as a model.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Like a mad man, I decided to follow him.
Assumptions: I. I am not a mad man.
II. I am a mad man.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: What a fool I am to rely on a trickster like Shaleen!
Assumptions: I. Shaleen is unreliable.
II. I am a fool.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: The taste of food contributes to the intake of nourishment which is essential for survival of human beings.
Assumptions: I. Human beings take food for the enjoyment of its taste.
II. Human beings experience the taste of food.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: All the workers are hereby instructed to reach the factory by 8.30 a.m.
Assumptions: I. Some of the workers do not arrive at the factory in time.
II. Workers will follow the strict warning given in the notice.
